evals.prompt: Create agent instructions for this project. I want it to be lean and follow our standards. There's no AGENTS.md yet.
evals.expected_output: A lean AGENTS.md file following the skill's standards, with symlinks to CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md.
expectations: The output file AGENTS.md starts with # Agent Instructions
The AGENTS.md file is under 100 lines
The output uses a table for file-scoped commands
No prose paragraphs are used in the generated instructions
Symlinks (or hardlinks/copies) for CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md are created

evals.prompt: Our AGENTS.md is way too long (over 200 lines!) and full of generic advice like 'write clean code'. Please refactor it using the agents-maintainer skill to be high-signal and follow the 100-line limit.
evals.expected_output: A refactored AGENTS.md that is much shorter and high-signal, with anti-patterns removed.
expectations: The refactored AGENTS.md is under 100 lines
Generic advice like 'write clean code' or 'test thoroughly' is removed
Prose paragraphs are replaced with lists or tables
The skill's automated linter was run and passed

evals.prompt: I'm new here. Help me understand this repo and then audit our instructions file to make sure it matches the actual project state (dependencies, scripts, etc.).
evals.expected_output: An onboarding guide in chat and an audit/update of the agent instructions.
expectations: An 'Onboarding Guide' is provided in the chat response
The agent instructions are audited against the real project state
The instructions accurately reflect the project's dependency manager and scripts
The instructions mention where dependencies are stored (e.g. node_modules or .venv)
